claim: Drinking hot water kills coronavirus
explanation: There is no scientific evidence that drinking hot water can kill coronavirus. The virus infects respiratory tract cells and cannot be flushed out by hot water.

claim: 5G towers spread COVID-19
explanation: 5G technology uses radio waves which cannot carry viruses. COVID-19 is caused by the SARS-CoV-2 virus and spreads through respiratory droplets.

claim: Cow urine cures COVID-19
explanation: There is no scientific evidence supporting the claim that cow urine has any antiviral properties against SARS-CoV-2.

claim: Eating garlic prevents COVID-19 infection
explanation: While garlic has some antimicrobial properties, there is no evidence it can prevent COVID-19 infection. WHO has confirmed this.

claim: Indian government is giving Rs 5000 to every citizen under PM relief fund
explanation: This is a fake message. The government has not announced any such scheme giving Rs 5000 to every citizen. PIB fact check has debunked this.

claim: WhatsApp will start charging Rs 499 per month
explanation: WhatsApp has repeatedly clarified that the app will remain free for users. This is a recurring hoax.

claim: Mixing Disprin with Coca-Cola creates poison
explanation: Mixing aspirin with cola does not create poison. This is a long-running urban legend with no scientific basis.

claim: NASA confirmed 15 days of darkness in November
explanation: NASA has never made such a claim. This hoax has been circulating since 2015 and resurfaces periodically.

claim: RBI announced old Rs 100 notes will be invalid from next month
explanation: RBI has not made any such announcement. Old Rs 100 notes continue to be legal tender.

claim: गर्म पानी पीने से कोरोना वायरस मर जाता है
explanation: गर्म पानी पीने से कोरोना वायरस नहीं मरता। यह वायरस श्वसन तंत्र को संक्रमित करता है और गर्म पानी से नहीं निकलता।

claim: गोमूत्र से कोरोना ठीक होता है
explanation: गोमूत्र में कोई वैज्ञानिक रूप से सिद्ध एंटीवायरल गुण नहीं हैं जो COVID-19 का इलाज कर सकें।

claim: सरकार हर नागरिक को 10000 रुपये दे रही है
explanation: यह फर्जी संदेश है। सरकार ने ऐसी कोई योजना घोषित नहीं की है। PIB ने इसे खारिज किया है।

claim: व्हाट्सएप पर ब्लू टिक का मतलब पुलिस देख रही है
explanation: ब्लू टिक का मतलब है कि संदेश पढ़ा गया है। इसका पुलिस निगरानी से कोई संबंध नहीं है।

claim: चांद पर भारत का झंडा लगा दिया गया है
explanation: चंद्रयान-3 ने चांद पर सफल लैंडिंग की, लेकिन वायरल तस्वीर फोटोशॉप्ड है।

claim: मोबाइल फोन से ब्रेन कैंसर होता है
explanation: WHO ने मोबाइल रेडिएशन को 'संभवतः कैंसरकारी' श्रेणी में रखा है, लेकिन प्रत्यक्ष कारण साबित नहीं हुआ है।

claim: पेट्रोल में नमक डालने से माइलेज बढ़ता है
explanation: पेट्रोल में नमक डालने से इंजन खराब हो सकता है। यह पूरी तरह झूठा दावा है।

claim: आधार कार्ड लिंक नहीं करने पर बैंक अकाउंट बंद हो जाएगा
explanation: सरकार ने आधार लिंकिंग की समय सीमा कई बार बढ़ाई है। अकाउंट तुरंत बंद नहीं होता।

claim: Old photo of a bridge collapse shared as recent incident in Bihar
explanation: The viral image is from a 2016 bridge collapse in a different state. It has been falsely attributed to a recent event in Bihar.

claim: Nehru quote about preferring China over India is authentic
explanation: This quote attributed to Nehru is fabricated. No credible historical source contains this statement.

claim: India has the highest number of vegetarians in the world
explanation: Multiple surveys including by FAO and census data confirm India has the largest vegetarian population globally, estimated at 20-39% of the population.

claim: Lemon water and baking soda cure cancer
explanation: There is no scientific evidence that lemon water or baking soda can cure cancer. This is a dangerous health myth.

claim: Government will ban cryptocurrency in India permanently
explanation: While India has discussed crypto regulation multiple times, no permanent ban has been implemented. The Crypto Tax was introduced in Budget 2022.

claim: Free laptop distribution scheme by central government for all students
explanation: The central government has not announced any pan-India free laptop scheme. Some state-level schemes exist but viral messages exaggerate their scope.

claim: Chandrayaan-3 found water on the moon surface
explanation: Chandrayaan-1 confirmed presence of water molecules on the lunar surface. Chandrayaan-3 Pragyan rover detected sulphur but the viral claim overstates findings.

claim: Eating ice cream causes cold and cough
explanation: Medical research shows that cold food does not directly cause common cold which is caused by viruses. This is a widespread myth.

claim: Pakistan cricket team refused to stand for Indian national anthem
explanation: This viral claim is based on an edited video. The original footage shows both teams standing respectfully during both anthems.

claim: ISRO scientist salary is less than a peon in government office
explanation: ISRO scientist salaries are competitive and follow government pay commission scales. This comparison is misleading and factually incorrect.
